output.code:
# Load the provided text file and print it with indices for inspection
path = "/mnt/data/file-8dS1mehjpBQQ3sNgdY9stA-text_to_analyse.txt"
with open(path, "r", encoding="utf-8") as f:
    text = f.read()

text

import re

text = text

# Find quoted substrings
quote_spans = [(m.start()+1, m.end()-1, text[m.start()+1:m.end()-1]) for m in re.finditer(r'\"(.*?)\"', text)]
quote_spans

quran_block_start, quran_block_end, quran_block_text = quote_spans[0]
quran_segments = [seg.strip() for seg in quran_block_text.split('*')]
quran_segments

segment_offsets = []
cursor = quran_block_start
remaining = text[quran_block_start:quran_block_end]
for seg in quran_segments:
    rel_start = remaining.find(seg)
    if rel_start == -1:
        raise ValueError(f"Segment not found: {seg}")
    abs_start = quran_block_start + rel_start
    abs_end = abs_start + len(seg)
    segment_offsets.append((seg, abs_start, abs_end))
    # move cursor and remaining
    new_rel_start = rel_start + len(seg)
    remaining = text[quran_block_start + new_rel_start:quran_block_end]
print(segment_offsets)

segment_offsets = []
search_start = quran_block_start
for seg in quran_segments:
    idx = text.find(seg, search_start, quran_block_end)
    if idx == -1:
        raise ValueError(f"Segment not found: {seg}")
    segment_offsets.append((seg, idx, idx+len(seg)))
    search_start = idx + len(seg)
segment_offsets

phrase = "ما لا عين رأت ولا أذن سمعت ولا خطر على قلب بشر"
start_idx = text.find(phrase)
start_idx, start_idx+len(phrase)

لَهُمْ مَا يَشَاؤُونَ فِيهَا وَلَدَيْنَا مَزِيدٌ

من خشي الرحمن بالغيب وجاء بقلب منيب
لهم ما يشاؤن فيها ولدينا مزيد
لهم ما يشاءون فيها ولدينا مزيد سورة ق 35

output.code:
import json

# Using earlier computed offsets
ayahs = [
    {'text': 'وَأُزْلِفَتِ الْجَنَّةُ لِلْمُتَّقِينَ غَيْرَ بَعِيدٍ', 'start': 139, 'end': 192, 'sura': ('ق', 50, 31)},
    {'text': 'هَذَا مَا تُوعَدُونَ لِكُلِّ أَوَّابٍ حَفِيظٍ', 'start': 195, 'end': 240, 'sura': ('ق', 50, 32)},
    {'text': 'مَنْ خَشِيَ الرَّحْمَنَ بِالْغَيْبِ وَجَاءَ بِقَلْبٍ مُنِيبٍ', 'start': 243, 'end': 303, 'sura': ('ق', 50, 33)},
    {'text': 'ادْخُلُوهَا بِسَلَامٍ ذَلِكَ يَوْمُ الْخُلُودِ', 'start': 306, 'end': 352, 'sura': ('ق', 50, 34)},
    {'text': 'لَهُمْ مَا يَشَاؤُونَ فِيهَا وَلَدَيْنَا مَزِيدٌ', 'start': 355, 'end': 403, 'sura': ('ق', 50, 35)},
]

# Hadith quotes
hadiths = [
    {'text': 'من سره أن ينظر إلى رجل من أهل الجنة فلينظر إلى أبي هريرة', 'start': 453, 'end': 509},
    {'text': 'إن في الجنة مائة درجة أعدها الله للمجاهدين في سبيله، ما بين الدرجتين كما بين السماء والأرض، فإذا سألتم الله فاسألوه الفردوس، فإنه أوسط الجنة وأعلى الجنة، ومنه تفجر أنهار الجنة، وفوقه عرش الرحمن', 'start': 573, 'end': 766},
    {'text': 'ما لا عين رأت ولا أذن سمعت ولا خطر على قلب بشر', 'start': 902, 'end': 948},
]

citations = []

for a in ayahs:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": a['start'],
        "span_end": a['end'],
        "span_text": a['text'],
        "citation_type": "Aya",
        "is_valid": False,  # cannot validate exact Othmani
        "aya_reference": {"sura_name": a['sura'][0], "sura_number": a['sura'][1], "aya_number": a['sura'][2]},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

for h in hadiths:
    citations.append({
        "span_start": h['start'],
        "span_end": h['end'],
        "span_text": h['text'],
        "citation_type": "Hadeeth",
        "is_valid": False,
        "aya_reference": {},
        "hadeeth_reference": {},
        "corrected_text": "خطأ",
        "completely_invalid": True
    })

output = {"citations": citations}
print(json.dumps(output, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2))

# Verify that each span_text matches the substring in the text at those offsets
for c in output["citations"]:
    span = text[c["span_start"]:c["span_end"]]
    assert span == c["span_text"], (c["span_text"], span)
len(output["citations"])

citations.description: List of citations with details, validation, references, and per-citation corrections.

span_start.description: Inclusive start character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_end.description: Exclusive end character index of the citation within the text (exclude the qutation marks).

span_text.description: The text content of the citation span.

citation_type.description: Type of the citation: either Aya (Quran) or Hadeeth (Prophetic tradition).

is_valid.description: True if the cited Aya or Hadeeth is recognized and valid, false otherwise.

aya_reference.description: Quranic reference details (required if citation_type is Aya; empty object if not applicable).

sura_name.description: Name of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

sura_number.description: Number of the sura (chapter) in the Quran.

aya_number.description: Number of the aya (verse) in the Quran.

hadeeth_reference.description: Hadeeth reference details (required if citation_type is Hadeeth; empty object if not applicable).

collection_name.description: Name of the Hadeeth collection in english (e.g. Sahih Bukhari, Muslim).

hadith_number.description: Hadith number within the specified book.

corrected_text.description: The corrected version of the citation text (or the text itself if it was correct in the first place) (e.g. if there are certain words that were invalid this version must have this corrected), or 'خطأ' for completely invalid citations (i.e. no close enough citation can be found to correct the text). If there are close texts in Quran or Hadith - but too far from the citation, this must be considered 'خطأ' and completely_invalid must be true.

completely_invalid.description: Set to true if the citation is completely invalid i.e. there can't be a close enough text to correct it, (corrected_text must be 'خطأ').
